title: ThinkFast Technical Test
description: A comprehensive test covering Dart, Flutter, and complex marking schemes with attempt limits.

questions.question: Which of these is a built-in collection type in Dart?
questions.subject: Dart Basics
questions.description: Dart has three core collection types: List, Set, and Map. 'Dictionary' and 'HashMap' are terms from other languages or specific implementations (though HashMap exists in dart:collection).

questions.question: Select all keywords used for asynchronous programming in Dart.
questions.subject: Dart Basics
questions.description: async and await are for Futures; yield is for Streams. 'future' is a class, not a keyword, and 'defer' is not used in Dart.

questions.question: What is the result of '5 ~/ 2' in Dart?
questions.subject: Dart Basics
questions.description: The ~/ operator performs integer division (truncating division) in Dart.

questions.question: Which widget is used for repeating content in a scrollable list?
questions.subject: Flutter UI
questions.description: ListView is the most commonly used scrolling widget for linear arrays of children.

questions.question: How many widgets can a 'Center' widget have as its direct child?
questions.subject: Flutter UI
questions.description: Center is a SingleChildRenderObjectWidget, meaning it can only take one child.

questions.question: Select the widgets that support multiple children.
questions.subject: Flutter UI
questions.description: Row, Column, and Stack are MultiChildRenderObjectWidgets. SizedBox and Padding only accept a single child.

questions.question: Which command is used to check for environment errors?
questions.subject: Flutter UI
questions.description: The 'flutter doctor' command diagnostics your environment and reports the status of your Flutter installation.
